page: Amazon Web Services r8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2111.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2958.0. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates are also strong, measuring 34.01 GB/sec and 14.41 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average range, including a multi-core Geekbench score of 8306.0 and an uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 76.86 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerating tile and integer operations.

The instance exhibits specific performance tradeoffs, particularly in LLM inference tasks where text generation for a medium 7B model is weak at 8.69 tokens/sec, though prompt processing for the same model reaches an average speed of 68.39 tokens/sec. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which must be factored into the overall resource density and architectural planning. Qualitatively, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1 provides cost-efficient memory capacity for memory-bound workloads without requiring excessive compute scaling. This profile is suited for database operations, Redis deployments, memory-intensive text processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or high-speed baseline networking.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, it utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and provides an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain average. Advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512 are supported. A notable tradeoff is the weak text generation speed of 8.69 tokens/sec for medium LLM models. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and text processing where local storage is not a prerequisite.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and no local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 4 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for specialized acceleration
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-bound applications
